What's your goal today?

1. Hire us to train you or your team

We've helped individuals & teams raise millions, convince CEO's to spend billions, and inpire thousands to act. To discover how we can help you create killer presentations for high-stakes meetings:

  • Read our case studies and customer testimonials
  • Learn about us, our unique values, beliefs and quirks
  • Claim your FREE trial of our flagship course 'Decks for Decsion Makers'
Individuals: Claim FREE trial Teams: Book a Discovery Call
2. Learn how to create a killer presentation

Download a free copy of our Accelerator Kit. All the resources we have ever made (for FREE) including our Advanced Guide to Business Presentaitons eBook and our PowerPoint Template (with over 1,000 professionally designed slides). You’ll also be subscribed to our email newsletter and notified whenever we publish new articles or have something interesting to share.

Browse our articles, containing an amazing number of useful insights, techniques and tools. The content in these articles forms the beadrock of our methodology and course.

  • Advanced Guide to McKinsey Presentations
  • Pitch Decks & Funding Proposals
  • Complete Guide to Busienss Charts
  • The Best Presentation Tools
  • Conquering Public Speaking Anxiety
Download a copy of our Accelerator Kit
3. Contact Us

We help businesses worldwide. So contact us..

[BPM] Business Presentation Mastery: Prologue

Thanks for signing up for 'Lesson Zero' of SlideHeroes' signature business presentation training 'Decks for Decision Makers' (or DDM for short).

Really glad you are here. (If you have not had a chance to watch the lesson, you can see it here.)

And welcome to the 'Business Presentation Mastery' email series (or 'BPM', for short), where I will dive deeper into many of the ideas we touch on in Lesson Zero and throughout the DDM course.

DDM is used by employees at large companies (e.g. Walmart, Pfizer, npower, Swiss Re), financial service providers and investment banks (Goldman Sachs, Swiss Re, Travelers), Silicon Valley start-ups (OneLeap), non-profits (Teach for America), the public sector (US Department of Energy) and is part of the teaching curriculum at colleges in Canada and the United States.

Over the last few years we have had thousands of graduates.

Here’s what venture capitalists say about SlideHeroes:

And John Lushetsky, the Chief Operating Officer of the Office of Policy at the US Department of Energy, had this to say about DDM:

But for now though, don't worry about Decks for Decision Makers.

There is nothing for you to buy here.

Enjoy this BPM (Business Presentation Mastery) series, yours, on the house.

My expectation is that it is going to provide you with AMAZING value.

Oh, and btw...

Every email I send you that's part of this training, will have a subject line prefixed with [BPM], like this:

[BPM] Subject line (Part X)

This way you'll instantly know it's associated with this training series.

When you see that email hit your inbox each day, open it and read it.

You'll get one "bite sized" email from me each day for the next three weeks.

This is on purpose. So you can easily consume each email (lesson).

I'm going to show you a (very) powerful way to approach creating, writing and designing business presentations.

In fact this approach works so well that companies pay salaries in the hundreds of thousands of dollars to people who have these skills.

Just saying.

You can do this, too.

And I'm going to show you how.

So pay attention.

Because what I'll be exposing you to will REFRAME how you and your teams engage with your business audiences – be they your executives, your partners, your investors, your customers, or your employees.

In a few days I'll also reveal a "case study" where I have a bit of fun critiquing a presentation from a leading management consultancy. 

I'll be sharing the whole tear down with you.

Anyway...

Lot's more to come.

Strap yourself in.

Michael "See PS" Smith

P.S.

Got a question for you...

What is your biggest challenge with presentations?

You know, the part you struggle with the most.

Or put another way... If you could wake up tomorrow morning and be a ROCKSTAR at this one thing (in regards to presentations), what would that be?

HIT REPLY and let me know.

It would mean the world to me.

P.P.S.

Hungry for more?

Let’s not waste any time and jump right into the good stuff.

Here is a link to our Manifesto.

Advanced Guide to Creating McKinsey-style Business Presentations. At 10,000 words, it is the size of a short novel.

P.P.P.S.

Remember: If we don't add meaningful value to your life or the work you do, please use the unsubscribe link at the bottom of this email. No hard feelings. Promise.

Try our course for Free
